                                     PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




Private Access (ZPA)
Access Policy Configuration Examples
You can configure access policies to meet the specific needs of your organization. To learn more about
the different policy types and the order in which Private Access (ZPA) enforces policies, see About
Policies.
The following examples illustrate the best way to configure policies for a variety of scenarios:
    Allow any user access to any application segments or segment groups

     To allow any user access to any application segments or segment groups:
        1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
        2. Under Criteria, leave the Application Segments and Segment Groups fields blank with the
           SAML Attributes field set to Any SAML attribute from an IdP. By not selecting a specific
           application segment, segment group, or SAML attribute you are indicating that the policy rule
           should allow any user (from any IdP) access to any application segment and segment group.
        3. Click Save.




                                                                             ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   1
                                 PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




 Allow specific users access to specific segment groups

 This configuration is useful if you have a set of applications that you want to group together, and
 you want to specify who can access that set of applications. For example, you might have a set of
 applications that you want all users in your organization to access (i.e., an intranet application and
 a benefits application). For this example, you would set up your policy rule using this configuration
    1. Create a segment group that includes the applications you want to configure policies for.
    2. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
    3. Under Criteria:
           a. For Segment Groups, select the segment group you created in step 1.



                                                                          ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   2
                            PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




     b. For SAML Attributes, leave the field set to Any SAML attribute from an IdP for this
        example. This will apply the policy rule to any user from any IdP you have configured for
        user single sign-on (SSO) .

           If you had more than one IdP configured for your organization and you only
          wanted to apply this policy rule to a specific one, you would click Select IdP to
           choose it and then select any SAML attribute from the drop-down menu.

4. Click Save.




                                                                    ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   3
                                PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




.
In another example, you might have a set of applications that you only want users from a specific
department to access (i.e., HR applications that only users from the Sales department can
access). For this example, you would set up your policy rule using this configuration
   1. Create an segment group that includes the applications you only want users in the Sales
      department to have access to.
   2. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
   3. Under Criteria:
          a. For Segment Groups, select the segment group you created in step 1.
          b. For SAML Attributes:
                 i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the policy
                    rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
                ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use from the drop-down menu and provide
                    the proper entry for it.
   4. Click Save.




                                                                        ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   4
                                 PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




 .

 Allow specific users access to any application segments or segment groups

 This configuration is useful if you have a set of users (i.e., users from your IT organization) who
 must be able to access all applications in your organization.

                                                                          ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   5
                                 PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




 To allow specific users access to any application segments or segment groups:
    1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
    2. Under Criteria:
           a. Leave the Application Segments and Segment Groups fields blank. By not entering
              specific applications or segment groups you are indicating that the policy should apply
              to any application segments or segment groups.
           b. For SAML Attributes:
                  i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the policy
                     rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
                 ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use from the drop-down menu and provide
                     the proper entry for it.
    3. Click Save.

 Allow specific users access to specific application segments or segment groups

 To allow specific users access to specific application segments or segment groups:
    1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
    2. Under Criteria:
           a. Select the Application Segments or Segment Groups you've configured from the drop-
              down menus.
           b. For SAML Attributes:
                  i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the policy
                     rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
                 ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use (e.g., Group) from the drop-down menu
                     and provide the proper entry for it (e.g., Marketing Dept).
    3. Click Save.




                                                                         ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   6
                                PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




 Block specific users access to any application segments or segment groups

 To block specific users from accessing any application segments or segment groups:
    1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Block Access.
    2. Under Criteria:


                                                                      ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   7
                               PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




         a. Leave the Application Segments and Segment Groups fields blank. By not selecting any
            specific application segments or segment groups you are indicating that the policy
            should apply to any application segments or segment groups.
         b. For SAML Attributes:
                i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the policy
                   rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
               ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use (e.g., Group) from the drop-down menu
                   and provide the proper entry for it (e.g., Sales).
   3. Click Save.
Because policy rules are enforced in the order listed, if the users you want to block are covered
under another access policy rule which allows them access, that allow policy must be listed after
this block policy. For example, if you have two access policy rules, one allowing access and one
blocking, they must be listed in the following order:
  1. Block Policy: Blocking users within the Sales group (authenticating using a specific IdP) from
     any application segments or segment groups.




                                                                      ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   8
                            PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




2. Allow Policy: Allowing any other users (authenticating from any other IdP) access to any
   application segments or segment groups.




                                                                    ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   9
                               PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




For this example, the Rule Order within the Access Policy page would appear as follows:




                                                                     ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   10
                                  PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




 Block specific users access to specific application segments or segment groups

 You can block specific users from accessing specific applications and segment groups, however,
 this is only necessary under specific circumstances. By default, until you configure policy rules for
 applications, users are blocked from accessing any applications or segment groups. They can
 access applications and segment groups only if you explicitly configure policy rules allowing them
 to do so. So, configuring a policy rule to deny users access would only be useful for the following
 circumstances:
       The number of users being blocked from application segments or segment groups is
       relatively small compared to the number of users with access. For example, if your
       organization wants to make application segments or segment groups available to most users,
       but block just a few users, you would create an access policy blocking the specific users,
       then another access policy allowing any other users. In this example, you would set up your
       policy rules using this configuration
          1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Block Access.
          2. Under Criteria:
                 a. Select the Application Segments or Segment Groups you've configured from the
                    drop-down menus. In this example, the Internal Application was selected.
                 b. For SAML Attributes:
                        i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the
                           policy rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
                       ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use (e.g., Group) from the drop-down
                           menu and provide the proper entry for it, in this case the user group you want
                           to block (e.g., Temp Staff).
          3. Click Save.



                                                                           ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   11
                       PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




4. On the Access Policy page, click Add Rule again.
5. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
       a. Leave the Application Segments and Segment Groups fields blank. By not
          selecting a specific application segment or segment group you are indicating that
          the policy should apply to any application segment and segment group.
                                                               ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   12
                       PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




      b. For SAML Attributes:
             i. Click Select IdP and choose the same IdP configuration you included in step
                2b above.
            ii. Select Any SAML Attribute from the drop-down menu.
6. Click Save.




                                                               ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   13
                          PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




.
You want to allow a specific group of users access to application segments or segment
groups, but also want to block individual users within that group. In this example, you would
set up your policy rules using this configuration

                                                                  ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   14
                        PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




1. In the Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Block Access.
2. Under Criteria:
       a. Select the Application Segments or Segment Groups you've configured from the
          drop-down menus. In this example, the Firewall Apps Group was selected.
       b. For SAML Attributes:
              i. Click Select IdP and choose the IdP configuration you want include in the
                 policy rule. The IdP must be configured for user SSO.
             ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use, in this case the user in the group
                 you want to block (e.g., Email address), and provide the proper entry for it.
3. Click Save.




                                                                 ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   15
                      PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




4. On the Access Policy page, click Add Rule again.
5. In the new Add Access Policy window, for Rule Action, select Allow Access.
       a. Select the Application Segments or Segment Groups you've configured from the
          drop-down menus. In this example, the Firewall Apps Group was selected.
       b. For SAML Attributes:
                                                            ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   16
                       PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




            i. Click Select IdP and choose the same IdP configuration you included in step
               2b above.
           ii. Select the SAML attribute you want to use, in this case the group you want to
               allow (e.g., Group), and provide the proper entry for it (e.g., Sales).
6. Click Save.




                                                               ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   17
    PRIVATE ACCESS (ZPA) : ACCESS POLICY CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES




.




                                    ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   18
